overall_justification: While the majority of the edition focuses on other news, the article comparing "Gazeta de Commercio" and "Jornal do Commercio" strongly criticizes those advocating for low exchange rates (a key tenet of the Expansionist position). It links low exchange rates with harming creditors and disrupting established financial arrangements. The language used, such as "imoral," "arruinar a nação," and "esmagado," conveys a forceful rejection of Expansionist arguments.  Although there's no explicit mention of the Caixa de Conversão in this specific argument, the focus on exchange rates directly connects to the core of the monetary debate surrounding it.  The strength of this critique outweighs the more neutral or unrelated content in the rest of the edition.

supporting_evidence.quote: O imoral é advogar o cambio baixo no intuito de prejudicar os credores, porque ninguém aconselha um cambio inferior aquelle em que se baseiam as transacções...
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote directly attacks the core Expansionist argument for a lower exchange rate, framing it as immoral and harmful to creditors.

supporting_evidence.quote: Os valorisadores do papel-moeda querem valorisar o nosso systema monetario e financeiro e arruinar, por esse meio, nove décimos da nação, alterando situações, lesando contractos, espoliando direitos...
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This connects those advocating for paper money (often linked to Expansionist views) with the ruin of the nation, further strengthening the Orthodox critique.

supporting_evidence.quote: E' essa calamidade que a Caixa de Conversão vem impedir, é a propria existencia do Brasil que ella vem defender e salvar.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: While supporting the Caixa, this statement implicitly criticizes the existing financial system and those who benefit from it (implied to be Expansionists), aligning with an Orthodox desire for stability and order.
